summary: Design タロ to the "viral on character ALONE" bar (CEO 2037): 5 latest-2026 experts → 2 harsh viral/kid critics → director consolidates a single viral-grade IP spec (one signature, emotional hook, name, expression atlas, Grok prompt, palette rec, silhouette+read-test gate). Proposal for CEO.

director.character_name_final: タロ (Taro) — locked. 2-mora, ひらがな-readable by a 6yo, タメ口-warm, globally pronounceable, trademark-clean as an original creature (not a コトバ+探偵 descriptor). A name a 6yo can CALL is a name she bonds with. No change.
director.one_signature:
THE SIGNATURE IS ANATOMY, NOT A PROP — ONE mismatched-ear pair that freezes the brave-but-startled contradiction into a STILL silhouette. タロ has two oversized listening ears: ONE permanently pricked UP (word-hunting / besotted), ONE folded DOWN (the startle frozen into the body). This is the single ownable form. It survives 24px 1-bit WITH THE CAP REMOVED (the acceptance test both critics demand: hatless, still unmistakably タロ = a species, not a costume), it carries the whole personality with zero animation, and it doubles as the love-engine (both ears flatten on ビクッ, both prick on つかまえたっ).

How tail/glow/cap stay SUBORDINATE — they are demoted to non-signature roles by construction:
- CAP: demoted to the SECOND thing you notice. The too-big detective cap tipped over one eye stays as flavor + world-color-coding + a redraw cue, but it is NO LONGER the identity. If you remove it, タロ is still タロ. (This directly closes the skeptic's "bean in a hat = Halloween costume" flaw.)
- GLOW (#E0A85A): a COLOR-STATE, invisible in silhouette by design — the EARNED in-app 合格率 reward, never the viral signature (correcting the pedagogy-lens over-crowning of the glow). It cannot compete in a still frame because it is value, not shape.
- TAIL: cut entirely as a signature. A short inside-the-outline texture bump only (never past 1× body-radius); it puffs on startle for animation accent. Not a competing shape.
One form (mismatched ears) does four jobs: silhouette-ownable, hatless-survivable, the brave/startled emotion engine in a STILL pose, and 6yo-redrawable (one tall ear, one folded). That is the anti-committee discipline.
director.emotional_hook: The love-engine is ONE child-caused micro-loop, and the FLINCH — not the glow — is its shareable face: (1) a new English word appears → タロ does the びくっ full-body flinch (down ear flattens further, up ear jolts, cap tips over the one eye, tail puffs); (2) beat — the visible eye peeks back out from under the brim (…っ, the brave spirit reasserting); (3) the CHILD answers correctly → the honey-amber pride-glow blooms from タロ's chest outward. タロ's ONLY happy-state is glowing BECAUSE OF the kid's right answer — the child isn't praised by a teacher, the child made their tiny brave friend glow and stay alive. Portable origin line (meme-able without playing): 「タロは ことばを たべると ひかる。だから きみの『せいかい』で げんきになるんだっ。」 Pedagogy guard is structural: タロ flinches, peeks, hugs, and glows — it NEVER speaks, defines, or grades a word; on a wrong answer it stays besotted with the word and leans in (「…このことば、すきっ」), glow dims, never droops/frowns. The animation state machine makes grading impossible, not writer discipline.
director.why_viral_alone: Honest case: viral-on-character-alone is CONDITIONAL-NOW, becomes TRUE once the two critic flaws are fixed — and this spec fixes them. The 2026 pattern it rides is Mirumi (Yukai Engineering, CES Jan 2026, Kickstarter ~104× goal, ¥79.4M / 2,053 backers): the breakout went viral on ONE autonomous brave-but-startled micro-behavior (the shy peek), explicitly grounded in "a baby peeking from a parent's arms" — the exact need-a-partner love-engine タロ runs. Convergent proof: Twinkle-Twinkle and Crybaby go viral as a FROZEN OBJECT on a shelf, holding ONE emotional state with absolute consistency. Why the original spec was NOT yet viral (both critics, triangulated): (a) the signature lived in a removable HAT, so hatless タロ was a generic bean = every Sonny Angel/Jellycat/Momiji on the 2026 shelf; (b) the warmth + payoff lived only in a 0.6s glow animation, invisible in every static surface a stranger ever sees (favicon, sticker, plush, thumbnail) — the precise inverse of how Mirumi/Crybaby went viral as a still. This spec crosses to YES by (1) moving the signature into mismatched-ear ANATOMY that survives 24px hatless, and (2) making warmth STRUCTURAL in the face (worried-hopeful eyes + matte blush) so a frozen thumbnail is both unmistakably タロ AND huggable, and (3) promoting the FLINCH (a still-readable, "ヤダ"-class panic-sticker beat — Mirumi's proven share-driver) to sticker-#1, keeping the glow as the earned reward, not the crowned IP.
director.silhouette_spec:
Proportions (baby-schema, high-but-not-maxed, anti-uncanny): head-to-body = 1 : 1.1 (head slightly smaller than body so タロ can WALK and adventure — brave, not the helpless 1:1 maxed-chibi that triggers parent "too babyish" + the floaty-head uncanny). Body = one soft vertical egg (~1.05:1, taller than wide) so it never reads as a slime puddle or a ball. Two mismatched oversized ears = THE form: one pricked tall (word-hunting), one folded (startle), set low-and-wide. Eyes: two, large-but-not-doll (~22% head-width each), set LOW (below head midline), spaced wide; ONE eye shadowed by the cap brim. Short stub limbs, no fingers. Matte (not glossy) blush ovals. Cap = secondary read (oversized, tipped over one eye). Tail = inside-the-outline bump only.

What reads at 24px 1-bit (acceptance criteria, BOTH must pass before art spend): (i) HATLESS test — remove the cap; the asymmetric tall-ear/folded-ear pair alone must still read as タロ and as "brave-but-startled." If it reads as a generic blob hatless, REJECT — it's a costume, not an IP. (ii) The glow is INVISIBLE at 1-bit (it's value), so identity must carry without it. (iii) WITH the cap, the round-body + tilted-brim + one-tall-ear notch is the unmistakable favicon. Build artifact first: a black 24×24 favicon, rendered both hatted AND hatless, both unmistakably タロ.
expression_atlas: つかまえたっ！ — hero face: fist-up, both ears pricked, chest beaming, honey-glow blooming (the catchphrase + 合格率 reward moment)
びくっ — THE breakout/sticker-#1: cap jolts over both eyes, both ears flatten, body rigid, tail puff. Readable as a STILL pose; the Mirumi-flinch, the 'ヤダ'-class panic share-driver
…っ — shy peek: one eye emerges from under the brim, down-ear half-lifts (the brave spirit reasserting)
だいすきっ — hugging a single glowing letter-tile to its chest like treasure, eyes squeezed in bliss (besotted-collector identity, never defines the word)
むね、ぴかーっ — full pride-glow at max: tallest stance, cap pushed UP off the eye, both ears straight (the 'you did it' screenshot face)
しょんぼり — wrong-answer EMPATHY (NOT 'you're wrong'): ears soften, glow dims, leans in 「…このことば、すきっ」; replaces any ✓/✗ grade sticker
ねむい / おやすみっ — droopy ears, half-closed eyes (streak / daily-return)
おはようっ — both ears spring up, blink-bright (daily-return reward)
がんばれっ — cheering the CHILD, pom-arms up (encourages, never grades)
ぴかーん — idea spark: one ear flicks, tiny bulb glint (thinking/hint beat)
director.grok_prompt:
Original creature mascot character named Taro, full body, front view, standing, centered on plain flat cream background. A small soft round egg-shaped body, slightly taller than wide, no neck. TWO MISMATCHED oversized rounded listening ears — one ear pricked straight UP and alert, the other ear folded DOWN — this asymmetric ear pair is the defining feature. Large round low-set eyes spaced wide, gentle worried-hopeful expression, single highlight dot per eye, two small matte blush ovals on the cheeks, tiny mouth. Short stub arms and legs, no fingers. Wearing an oversized round detective cap tipped over one eye as a secondary accent, with a brass band. Body color dusty teal (#5E8B8B) with one darker cel shadow tone (#3E6566), cap warm brass (#C9A24B), face accents cream (#F2E8D5). Clean uniform 1px ink contour, slightly thicker on the outer silhouette, two-tone flat cel shading with hard-edged shadow blocks, flat color fields, four flat colors total, matte finish. Warm, huggable, instantly lovable as a still image, reads clearly as a tiny brave-but-startled creature. Adorable kawaii companion, child-friendly, premium hand-illustrated anime cel style.
Avoid: airbrush, soft gradients, smooth blur, painterly rendering, rendered fur or hair strands, ambient glow or halo, honey-amber body color, monocle, scarf, badge, long or visible tail, symmetric matching ears, bunny-tall or cat-pointed ears, slime, blob, ball, puddle, generic chibi, photorealism, 3D, text, watermark, drop shadow, busy background, more than four colors, checkmark, cross mark.
director.palette_recommendation: RECOMMEND the cool-cohesion HYBRID, NOT a warm body. Body = DUSTY-TEAL (#5E8B8B / shadow #3E6566) for コトバ探偵 world-cohesion AND so the body color stays DISTINCT from the glow. Cap = WARM BRASS (#C9A24B) as the single focal warm accent that pulls the eye and reads parent-friendly at rest. Honey-amber (#E0A85A) RESERVED 100% for the earned pride-glow STATE. Why over a warm body: a warm-honey body (Option B) makes body-color = glow-color, which KILLS the earned-glow payoff — the single best 合格率 beat. A cool creature that visibly BLOOMS warm-gold when the child succeeds is one ownable, screen-recordable transformation; that contrast IS the IP (Pikachu owns a color, タロ owns a color-CHANGE tied to the child's win). NOTE — this hybrid resolves the warmth concern via the FACE (worried-hopeful eyes + matte blush carry still-frame warmth), NOT via the body color, so cool-cohesion and frozen-thumbnail huggability coexist.
director.gate_plan:
Two hard gates, BOTH must pass BEFORE any art-batch spend, then a third before any merch:
1) SILHOUETTE / HATLESS test (the skeptic's kill-test, automated): render a black 24×24 1-bit favicon of タロ in two variants — WITH cap and WITHOUT cap. PASS only if hatless タロ is still unmistakably タロ and still reads "brave-but-startled" from the mismatched ears alone. If hatless = generic blob, REJECT and re-anatomize. (This is the line between an IP and a Halloween costume.)
2) REAL 6yo (RINA) + PARENT read-test on a STILL frame (not animation, not the bible): (a) the 6yo names the current emotion from the FACE ALONE within 2s, using only the legible set a 6-7yo reliably reads — happy / surprise / 「がんばってる」 (per Children's Emotions Database; subtle/contempt FAIL at age 6, so never teach on those); (b) the parent describes タロ as "encouraging" — never "creepy / anxious / gacha." HARD pedagogy flag: do NOT borrow Labubu's "anxious ugly-cute unease" — that is Gen-Z-collector psychology, reads as scary to a 6yo and untrustworthy to a parent. Warmth must DOMINATE; every startle resolves to bravery+warmth within the same beat.
3) BUILD-BLOCKER (verified in code, must fix before the above is even testable): the shipped build literally renders a SLIME named タロ — lib/features/quest/quest_screen.dart line 330 maps 'タロ'→assets/art/masters/slime.webp, and assets/art/npc/npc_slime_color.webp / npc_slime_grey.webp are the in-scene art. Until that pointer is the real タロ render, "does the child love タロ" is untestable and the honest answer is "she sees a slime" (which [[distinct-identity-not-ip-copy]] flags as exactly the DQ-monster identity we are trying to escape). Replace the slime pointer with art that passes gates 1+2 BEFORE any further wiring.
director.open_for_ceo:
Spec-freeze proposal — needs CEO adjudication on 4 points before build (do NOT auto-edit):
1) SIGNATURE PIVOT (the core decision): both harsh critics independently killed the cap-as-signature. I am moving the sole signature OFF the cap and INTO mismatched-ear ANATOMY (one up / one folded), demoting the cap to the second read. CONFIRM this pivot — it changes the locked "cap = the one signature" from the facet panel.
2) PALETTE: approve the cool-cohesion HYBRID (dusty-teal body #5E8B8B + brass cap #C9A24B + honey-amber #E0A85A RESERVED for the earned glow). The alternative (warm-honey body) is on the table but kills the earned-glow payoff — I recommend against it.
3) VIRAL SIGNATURE BEAT: I am promoting the FLINCH (びくっ) to sticker-#1 / the shareable IP beat, and keeping the glow as the earned 合格率 reward rather than the crowned signature (one facet had crowned the glow). CONFIRM.
4) MERCH MODEL: deterministic earned ことばコレクション + a NAMED VISIBLE assortment (gachapon/plush, pick-the-face), with ZERO sealed-random / spend-to-reveal — explicitly dodging the June-2026 Shanghai/Singapore/Australia blind-box backlash. CONFIRM the no-gacha lock.
Build-order ask: authorize the slime→タロ render replacement (quest_screen.dart:330 + npc_slime_*.webp) as the FIRST build step once 1–3 are confirmed; gates 1–2 run on that render before any expression-sheet spend. Files load-bearing: lib/features/quest/quest_screen.dart:330; assets/art/masters/slime.webp; assets/art/npc/npc_slime_color.webp; assets/art/npc/npc_slime_grey.webp.

designs.facet: Character design — iconic ownable silhouette + the single signature + baby-schema proportions
designs.latest_2026_reference: Pop Mart's breakout 2026 IP "Twinkle Twinkle" (the analyst-named Labubu successor, ~1B Xiaohongshu impressions, resale up to 30×) wins on a SINGLE warm honey glow + blush + ONE star-nose — not a feature-stack — proving the 2026 viral law is one-signature emotional warmth, not detail density (Jing Daily, Blindbox Daily, NEWS NEST, Feb–Jun 2026). Crybaby's parallel rise is built on ONE oversized-tear silhouette read as a bag-charm. The Disney/Creative Bloq silhouette doctrine: a character must be recognizable as a solid black shape BEFORE any interior detail (Mickey/Sonic test). These together kill the committee-creature (cap+tail+glow+monocle) and validate: the cap is the silhouette, the glow is the COLOR-state layer (not a shape), the tail is texture-only.
designs.contribution:
TARO SPEC — ownable in 1-bit at 24px, redrawable by a 6yo in 4 strokes.

THE ONE SIGNATURE = THE CAP (resolves the gate). タロ's silhouette IS "a too-big round detective cap tipped over one eye, sitting on a small round body." The cap is the only feature that survives the silhouette test as a SHAPE — it gives an asymmetric, instantly-redrawable outline (round body + offset round cap = a lopsided snowman = a 6yo draws two circles, one tilted). The tail and glow are deliberately SUBORDINATE and NON-silhouette:
- TAIL: a short stubby single curl, kept INSIDE the body's round mass — it is a texture/wiggle accent for animation (startle = tail puffs), never a shape that breaks the outline. In silhouette it reads as a tiny bump, not a competing feature. Rule: tail must never extend past 1× body-radius.
- GLOW (#E0A85A honey-amber): it is a COLOR/LIGHT STATE, not a geometric feature — it blooms as a soft ambient rim + the cap-brass-band lights up on the child's correct answer. In a 1-bit silhouette the glow is invisible (it's value, not shape), so it cannot compete with the cap. This is exactly the Twinkle-Twinkle move: warmth carried by light+blush, identity carried by one shape.

SILHOUETTE (the ownable form): NOT a known animal. Body = one soft vertical egg (taller than wide, ~1.05:1) so it never reads as a slime puddle or a ball. Two SMALL rounded ears set LOW and wide on the head (not bunny-tall, not cat-pointed) — just enough non-human bumps to say "original creature." The cap's asymmetric tilt is the whole identity. Negative space under the tilted brim (the one shadowed eye) is the second ownable cue.

BABY-SCHEMA PROPORTIONS (high-but-not-maxed, anti-uncanny):
- Head-to-body = 1 : 1.1 (head slightly smaller than body so it stands & adventures — NOT the 1:1 maxed-chibi that reads as helpless/infantile and triggers the parent "too babyish" and the uncanny floaty-head). This is the "brave-but-startled" body: competent enough to walk, small enough to need the kid.
- Eyes: TWO, large, round, set LOW (below head midline) and spaced WIDE — eye-size ~22% of head width each (large, not the 30%+ glassy-doll size that goes uncanny). ONE eye always shadowed by the cap brim = the asymmetry that makes him "a detective," and halves the doll-stare that creeps parents out.
- Blush: two small matte (not glossy) cheek ovals — the 2026 instant-cute multiplier, kept matte to stay warm-not-saccharine.
- Mouth: tiny, expressive, capable of a wide open "つかまえたっ！" gape (the catchphrase moment) — small at rest, big on joy.
- Limbs: short stub arms/legs, rounded, no fingers — keeps the redraw-by-6yo promise and the warm read.

THE BRAVE/STARTLED CONTRADICTION made VISUAL: rest pose = cap tilted forward, chin up, tiny chest out (brave). Startle pose = SAME body, cap pops UP off the eyes revealing both wide eyes + tail puff (the love-trigger). One rig, two poses, the cap does the acting — so the signature feature is also the emotion engine. The cap NEVER comes fully off (it's his courage); it only tips.

PEDAGOGY GUARD baked into art: タロ HOLDS/POINTS-AT words like fireflies he's caught, eyes sparkling — adoring, never pointing as a teacher. His correct-answer animation = the honey glow blooms FROM the child's answer TO タロ's cap-band (the pride flows child→creature), so he is celebrating the KID, never grading.
designs.the_one_thing: Commit the entire identity to THE TILTED OVERSIZED CAP as the one silhouette-defining signature, and demote glow to a color-state (invisible in silhouette) and tail to an inside-the-outline texture bump — because 2026's actual breakout (Twinkle Twinkle) proves virality comes from ONE shape carrying warmth, not a feature-stack, and the cap is the only タロ element that is simultaneously (a) silhouette-ownable, (b) 6yo-redrawable in 2 circles, (c) the brave/startled emotion engine (tips down=brave, pops up=startled), and (d) the 合格率 stage where the honey-glow blooms. One feature, four jobs — that is the anti-committee discipline the panel demanded.

designs.facet: Art direction — タロ's render language, palette, and merch-reproducible signature spec
designs.latest_2026_reference: June 2026: (1) the anime/illustration field now READS "AI-smooth / airbrushed gradient" as the cheap tell, and the premium human-made look is explicitly "crisp 1px ink contour + two-tone cel shadow blocks + flat color fields" — illustrators reach for clean cel precisely to signal "made by a person, not stamped by a flat shader" (clipstudio.net / pixflow.net / anifusion.ai, 2026). (2) Merch-viral plush winners (Jellycat, Sonny Angel, Momiji, Labubu-mini) win on SIMPLE shape + minimal color + photo-friendly palette, NOT complex features — and unit cost climbs per fabric panel and per color, so the signature must be embroiderable/flat-panel, not painted (leelinetoys.com / edntoy.com / stuffedparty.com, 2026). (3) The 2026 mascot silhouette law: "design at full complexity, verify it survives a 16px favicon in black-and-white; build around ONE dominant hook and make it impossible to miss" (logocrafter.app / ebaqdesign.com / semnexus.com, 2026).
designs.contribution:
タロ RENDER-LANGUAGE SPEC (buildable, cohesive with the refined-anime mains, merch-reproducible):

FORM (silhouette-first). A small round bean-body, ~1.3 heads tall, no neck. Big top-heavy head, tiny body = the "too-small body" contradiction read at a glance. Short stub limbs, NO fingers (plush-cheap, sticker-flat). Large round-pupil eyes with a single highlight dot; tiny "o" mouth that goes wobbly when startled. Two soft rounded ear/tuft shapes that DESCRIBE the silhouette — they are the "creature" read, not the cap.

THE ONE SIGNATURE (execution gate honored). The too-big detective cap tipped over ONE eye is the SOLE static signature feature. It is the only thing that breaks the round silhouette, it color-codes the character, it embroiders as one flat panel, and it is legible at 24px 1-bit. NO monocle, NO scarf, NO tail-tuft, NO badge — those are the committee-creature trap. The cap brim casting over one eye also visually encodes "easily startled / half-hiding" — form carries the personality, zero accessories needed.

THE GLOW IS A STATE, NOT A FEATURE. The honey-amber pride-glow (#E0A85A) is NOT in the resting design — it BLOOMS as a rim-light halo around タロ only on the child's correct answer (= the 合格率 signal). Because it is absent at rest, it never competes in the silhouette, and its scarcity is exactly what makes the "it lights up when I get it right" moment the screen-recordable viral beat (Stitch/Pikachu emotional-payoff mechanism).

RENDER RULES (anti-AI-slop, cohesive with mains).
- Linework: uniform-weight clean ink contour, ~1px scaled, slightly thicker on outer silhouette, thinner interior — NO sketchy/painterly line, NO line absorbed into gradient.
- Shading: exactly TWO tones per local color (base + ONE cel shadow block, hard edge). Optional ONE soft occlusion under the cap brim only. NO airbrush, NO ambient gradient bg bleed, NO rendered subsurface fur — matte cel, same as the mains.
- Color count: ≤4 flat fields total (body, cap, cap-band, eye/face accents) so it survives embroidery, screen-print, and a 4-color sticker.
- Fur is implied by 2-3 ink tufts at the silhouette edge, NOT by rendered hairs (AI-slop tell).

EXPRESSION KIT (word-besotted but never grades a word — pedagogy guard baked into ART). Build 6 cel face states reusable as flat overlays: ✶sparkle-adore (sees a word = treasure, NO checkmark/correct iconography on タロ itself), ✶startled (eyes wide, cap jolts up a notch, mouth wobble), ✶brave-puff (chest out, the brave-spirit beat), ✶tip-cap-thinking, ✶blink-idle, ✶amber-glow-celebration (the state above). Crucially, タロ's "correct" reaction is the AMBIENT amber bloom + adore-sparkle — タロ never displays ✓/✗ or points at "the right answer," so the art itself enforces "never grades a word."

PALETTE — two options, with a virality recommendation:
• OPTION A (cohesion-led) — body in DUSTY-TEAL (#5E8B8B / shadow #3E6566), cap in BRASS-BROWN (#9A6B3F / band #C9A24B), face accents cream (#F2E8D5). Pro: locks タロ into the dusty-teal+brass "コトバ探偵" world; instantly reads as belonging. Con: teal creatures are a slightly crowded 2026 lane; lower focal pop on a feed thumbnail.
• OPTION B (focal-led) — body in WARM HONEY/AMBER (#E0A85A / shadow #B97F3C), cap in DEEP DUSTY-TEAL (#2F4F4F), face cream. Pro: maximum thumbnail pop + warmth (parent-acceptable, "huggable"); honey body = instant emotional warmth. Con: the body color then equals the glow color, KILLING the earned-glow payoff (the single best viral beat).

RECOMMENDATION (virality): A HYBRID that takes A's body + B's warmth discipline. Body = DUSTY-TEAL (#5E8B8B) for world-cohesion AND so it stays distinct from the glow; cap = WARM BRASS (#C9A24B) as the focal warm accent that pulls the eye and gives parent-friendly warmth at rest; honey-amber (#E0A85A) RESERVED 100% for the earned pride-glow state. This is the highest-ceiling config: a cool-bodied creature that literally turns warm-gold when the child succeeds is a single, ownable, screen-recordable transformation — that contrast IS the IP. (Pikachu/Stitch own a color; タロ owns a color-CHANGE tied to the child's win.)
designs.the_one_thing: Make the honey-amber (#E0A85A) a STATE the creature transforms INTO on the child's correct answer — not a static body color or a permanent feature. Give タロ a cool dusty-teal resting body and a single signature (cap-over-one-eye), so that the warm-gold pride-glow is rare, earned, and tied to the 6yo's success. A cool creature that visibly blooms warm when the child is right is one ownable, screen-recordable transformation — that contrast is the viral IP, and it bakes the 英検 pedagogy guard into the art (タロ celebrates the CHILD, never grades the word).
sources: https://www.clipstudio.net/how-to-draw/archives/162911 — 2026 cel-shading craft: two-tone shadow blocks, 1px ink contour, flat color fields (vs de-emphasized painterly line)
https://pixflow.net/blog/5-best-anime-ai-art-generator-tools-for-fan-art-and-gaming-in-2026/ — 2026: premium look = strong line art + cel shading, avoiding the generic 'AI smooth' tell
https://anifusion.ai/style/cel-shading-anime-style-generator/ — 2026 definition of clean cel: 2-tone shadows, 1px ink contours, flat fields
https://www.logocrafter.app/blog/best-mascot-logos — 2026 mascot silhouette law: reads at 16px favicon, ONE dominant visual hook
https://www.ebaqdesign.com/blog/brand-mascots — 2026: simplify to 2-3 distinctive traits, silhouette recognizable in black-and-white
https://semnexus.com/app-icon-design-2026-conversion-patterns/ — 2026: design at full complexity, verify it survives the favicon silhouette
https://www.leelinetoys.com/plush-toy-construction-methods/ — 2026 plush production: flat-panel embroidery/screen-print, unit cost climbs per panel and per color → few colors, simple shape
https://edntoy.com/blog/top-pop-toy-characters-trending-in-2026 — 2026 pop-toy lanes incl. 'healing plush'; simple happy-face winners (Jellycat/Sonny Angel/Momiji)
https://stuffedparty.com/the-latest-plushie-announcements-whats-new-and-hot-in-2025-2026/ — 2026: photo-friendly color/texture beats complex features; mini bag-charm to couch-buddy size range

designs.facet: Emotional hook / narrative — the love-engine, name, catchphrase, and meme-able signature gags for タロ
designs.latest_2026_reference: Mirumi (Yukai Engineering, breakout toy of 2026) went viral on ONE behavior: it FLINCHES at a sudden noise, then PEEKS back up with shy wide-eyed curiosity — analysts call it "the Labubu of 2026," 65% of Gen Z collectors say they want "companionship from the item" (Heyup/Athlon/PitchOnNet, Jun 2026). Crybaby (Pop Mart, >1B yuan 2025–26, Tears Launch Project plush Feb 2026) proves the inverse: ONE signature emotional state held across every variant = a consistent, collectible identity (Athlon/PopBoxss, Jun 2026). Both confirm: the IP is ONE repeatable micro-reaction, not a feature pile.
designs.contribution:
LOCK THE NAME — keep タロ. It's 2-mora, ひらがな-readable by a 6yo, タメ口-warm, globally pronounceable (Taro), and trademark-clean as an original creature (not コトバ + 探偵 descriptor). The stronger-alt test (ミル/ココ/ナゾ) all lose to タロ on the one axis that matters: a 6yo can CALL it, and a name you can call is a name you bond with. Keep タロ.

THE LOVE-ENGINE, MADE CONCRETE (the single signature feature, per the execution gate):
タロ's whole IP is ONE micro-moment — the "ビクッ→…っ" — the exact Mirumi flinch-then-peek, but TURNED INTO THE GAMEPLAY beat:
  1. A new English word appears on screen → タロ does a tiny full-body BIKUTT (startle): cap jolts down over the one eye, tail pops stiff. (brave-spirit body betrays it)
  2. Beat. The visible one eye peeks back out from under the cap. (the brave spirit reasserts — "…だいじょうぶっ")
  3. The child answers correctly → the honey-amber pride-glow (#E0A85A) blooms from タロ's chest outward. (合格率 signal = タロ being PROUD OF YOU, not of itself)
This is the entire mechanism in one loop: タロ is brave but its too-small body flinches first → it visibly chooses courage → and its ONLY reward-state is glowing because of the CHILD. The kid isn't praised by a teacher; the kid made their tiny brave friend glow. That is the "feel needed" hook (Stitch/Pikachu/ちいかわ) fused to the 英検 correct-answer signal — game and pedagogy in one gesture, with ZERO risk of タロ ever defining/grading a word (it never speaks the answer; it only flinches, peeks, and glows).

CATCHPHRASE (locked): 「ことば、つかまえたっ！」 — used ONLY at the glow moment, never before an answer (so it can never leak/grade). Add ONE paired startle-line for the flinch: 「ひゃっ…だいじょぶっ」 (the two-beat of the duality in four もじ). These two lines ARE the brave↔jumpy duality as audio.

3 SIGNATURE GAGS (sticker/meme-ready, each survives a 24px 1-bit silhouette = readable as pure SHAPE):
  1. 「ぼうし、ずれるっ」 — the startle knocks the too-big cap fully over BOTH eyes; タロ freezes blind, tail rigid. Silhouette: round body + cap-over-face + stiff tail = instantly readable. (the flinch gag — the most shareable; this is タロ's "Mirumi flinch")
  2. 「ことば、だいすきっ」 — タロ hugs a single glowing letter-tile to its chest like treasure, eyes squeezed shut in bliss. Silhouette: body curled around a square. (the love-of-words gag — establishes the besotted-collector identity without EVER defining the word)
  3. 「むね、ぴかーっ」 — the full pride-glow at max: タロ stands tallest, chest beaming, cap finally pushed UP off the eye. Silhouette: small body + big radial glow = the "you did it" reaction sticker. (the reward gag — the one parents screenshot)
These three are ONE creature in three states of the SAME duality (hidden / besotted / brave-proud) — not four bolted-on features. They are the blind-box variant logic Crybaby proved: same identity, repeatable, collectible.

1-LINE ORIGIN THAT TRANSCENDS THE STORY (the thing people repeat without playing the game):
「タロは、ことばを たべると ひかる。だから、きみの『せいかい』で げんきになるんだっ。」 — "タロ glows when it eats words. So it's YOUR right answers that keep it alive." It reframes every correct answer as feeding/saving your friend. Portable, meme-able, and it never touches what a word MEANS — only that getting one right makes タロ glow. That single sentence makes a stranger want to protect タロ before they've read one case.
designs.the_one_thing: Build タロ as ONE micro-moment, not a creature with features: the "ビクッ→peek→むねぴかーっ" loop, where タロ's ONLY happy-state is glowing BECAUSE OF the child's correct answer. 2026's two biggest IPs (Mirumi's flinch-peek, Crybaby's one held expression) both prove the IP is a single repeatable reaction held with absolute consistency — and here that reaction IS the 英検 correct-answer signal. Make the flinch adorable and the glow earned-by-the-kid, and タロ is viral on character alone while being pedagogically incapable of ever grading a word.

designs.facet: Merch / IP flywheel producer — the one-glance asset, the 1:1 sticker pack, and the kid-safe collectible loop
designs.latest_2026_reference: (1) Mascot-design state-of-the-art, Ziggle 2026 ("How to Create a Mascot," 2026): the winning mascot has ONE dominant hook that is the first thing anyone names (Mickey ears, Pikachu's bolt-tail); "every line you add is a line that becomes invisible at 32×32 px or a line that a five-year-old cannot draw from memory" — this dated 2026 finding IS the panel's one-signature gate, externally confirmed. (2) ちいかわ LINE-stamp cadence (LINE STORE / dポイント collab, Feb 24 2026; "moving stamp vol.3," 24 expressions): proven that the *unit of virality* in Japan is a 16–24-face EXPRESSION PACK, refreshed in named sub-series ("ハチワレ多," "うさぎ多"), free-distributed via brand collabs as the acquisition channel. (3) Anti-gambling pressure, June 2026 (The Conversation "Gambling for children…", iGamingToday, Law Society Journal; Shanghai bans blind-box sale under-8, Singapore/Australia moving to regulate): the adult blind-box register is becoming legally radioactive for a 6yo product — must NOT copy it. (4) Pokémon GO sticker economy (Serebii/PokémonGOHub, GO Fest Copenhagen 2026): the kid-safe collectible model = stickers EARNED via research/gifts/buddy/achievement, deterministically, not bought sealed-and-random — this is the flywheel to copy instead of Pop Mart.
designs.contribution:
THE MERCH FLYWHEEL FOR タロ (honouring the ONE-signature gate):

1) THE ONE-GLANCE ASSET = THE TOO-BIG CAP TIPPED OVER ONE EYE. Not the glow, not a tail, not a monocle — pick ONE and the cap wins because: it survives a 1-bit 24px silhouette (a round body + an oversized angled brim reads instantly, like Mickey's ear-circles); it encodes the WHOLE character in one shape (too-big = small startled body inside a brave detective's job = the love-engine, visible without animation); a 6yo CAN draw it from memory (circle + tilted brim + one peeking eye). The honey-amber pride-glow (#E0A85A) is NOT a second signature — it is a STATE, not a shape: it lives only in-app/animated as the 合格率 reward and is deliberately ABSENT from static silhouette merch. That keeps us on the right side of the committee-creature trap. Silhouette test artifact to build first: a black 24×24 favicon of タロ that is unmistakably タロ with zero internal detail.

2) THE STICKER PACK THAT SHIPS 1:1 (16 + a 24-face moving vol.2, ちいかわ cadence). The in-app expression set IS the LINE pack — same art, zero redraw. Lock these 16 to double as in-game UI emotes AND the launch pack, each tied to a real app moment so they're earned, not decorative:
  - つかまえたっ！ (catchphrase, fist-up, glow) — the hero face
  - びくっ (startled, cap jumps) — the love-engine, the breakout-meme face (this is the ちいかわ「ヤダ」-class panic sticker that drives shares)
  - …っ (peeking from under the brim, shy)
  - だいすきっ (hugging a word-card like treasure)
  - ねむい / おはようっ (streak/daily-return)
  - がんばれっ (cheering the child, NOT grading)
  - しょんぼり (cap droops — wrong-answer EMPATHY, never "you're wrong")
  - ぴかーん (idea), どきどき, ぐっ (thumbs), ことばコレクション (showing a caught word), など to 16.
  Voice baked into stamp text: ひらがな-only, タメ口, 語尾「…っ」. Hard 英検 guard: NO sticker ever shows a word's meaning, a ✓/✗ grade, or タロ "knowing" an answer — he REACTS to the child's win, he never adjudicates. "しょんぼり" replaces any "wrong" stamp.

3) WHAT MAKES IT COLLECTIBLE/SHAREABLE WITHOUT THE GAMBLING REGISTER (Pokémon-GO model, not Pop-Mart): タロ's collectible layer = ことばコレクション — タロ "catches" a word the CHILD masters and it becomes an EARNED sticker/badge with タロ's reaction face. Deterministic, achievement-gated, parent-legible ("she earned 40 words"), zero sealed-random purchase, zero spend-to-reveal — this dodges the June-2026 Shanghai/Singapore/Australia blind-box backlash entirely. Physical extension is the same logic: a gachapon/plush range sold as a NAMED, VISIBLE assortment (you see タロ's 6 expressions on the shelf and pick the びくっ one) — collectible by completion-desire, never by concealment. Acquisition/virality channel = the ちいかわ×dポイント play: free LINE stamp via a parent-brand collab as the top-of-funnel that pulls families into the app.

4) BUILDABLE NOW (¥0): commission the 16 expression sheet ONCE → it is simultaneously app emotes, the LINE pack, gachapon faces, and the 24px favicon. One asset, four products = the flywheel's efficiency.
designs.the_one_thing: Commit the too-big cap tipped over one eye as タロ's SOLE silhouette signature, and demote the amber glow to an in-app-only STATE (never a static merch shape) — because the 2026 mascot evidence is blunt: the character that goes viral on character alone is the one a 6yo can redraw from memory and that survives a 24px black silhouette, and that is impossible if cap + tail + glow + monocle all compete. One shape that encodes the brave-but-startled love-engine (small body lost inside a big detective's hat) is the entire IP; everything else is animation and reward, not identity.

designs.facet: 2026 kids-learning character pedagogy lens (age 6+): does the viral design still WORK as a warm, emotion-readable learning companion that keeps the "never wrong about words" guard, parent-trust, and the clever-peer feel?
designs.latest_2026_reference: Mirumi (Yukai Engineering, Tokyo) — the breakout viral Japanese creature of 2026: CES Las Vegas debut Jan 2026; Kickstarter raised ¥79.4M ($500k+) from 2,053 backers vs a ¥765k goal (~104×). It went viral on ONE behavior — a shy, autonomous "peek" — explicitly grounded by CEO Aoki in "the moment a baby, held in a parent's arms, quietly peeks through a gap and makes eye contact with a stranger." This dates and PROVES the panel's two locks at once: (1) single-signature beats the feature-pile, and (2) the brave-but-startled / "needs a partner" love-engine is exactly what is selling RIGHT NOW. Convergent: 2026 EdTech mascot research (Whizzy/Masko) — best learning characters "own ONE clear emotional register," kids must ID them from a 24px notification icon; and child-development data (Children's Emotions Database / NCBI 2026) — 6-7yos read happy/sad/surprise reliably but FAIL on subtle/disgust/contempt, so the teaching emotion must be one of the legible three.
designs.contribution:
PEDAGOGY-LOCKED SPEC for タロ so the viral creature still teaches a 6yo:

1. THE SIGNATURE IS A TEACHING CHANNEL, NOT DECORATION. The honey-amber pride-glow (#E0A85A) must be the ONE signature kept (satisfies the exec gate — drop cap-tip/tail/monocle as competing signatures; the over-one-eye cap stays only as silhouette, not as a second emotional beat). Bind the glow 1:1 to the child's correct answer = the in-game 合格率 made VISIBLE and felt. This is the Mirumi move: one autonomous, emotion-true micro-behavior the child causes. Spec the glow on the legible axis 6yos can read: warm bloom = correct (surprise→joy), dim-and-lean-closer = "try again with me" (NEVER sad/scared, which reads as punishment to a 6yo and breaks parent-trust).

2. THE "NEVER WRONG ABOUT WORDS" GUARD, MADE BEHAVIORAL. タロ ADORES the word, never adjudicates it. On a wrong answer タロ does NOT droop, frown, or correct — it stays besotted with the WORD ("…このことば、すきっ"), glow dims slightly, and it leans in. The child re-tries; only the child's choice flips the glow. Pedagogically this keeps error blame-free (2026 child-AI scaffolds: companion models persistence, never judges) and structurally makes it impossible for タロ to grade/define — the guard is enforced by the animation state machine, not by writer discipline.

3. STARTLE = THE NEED HOOK, BUDGETED. The brave-but-startled beat (the love-engine) fires on NEW/unseen words and case-reveals — タロ flinches, then puffs up brave because the child is there. Cap it: startle is an ENTRY emotion (new word) and joy/glow is the REWARD emotion (mastered word). Never startle on a wrong answer (that would punish error). This makes the 6yo feel needed WITHOUT making English feel scary — the parent-trust line.

4. CLEVER PEER, NOT PET — via WORD-DELIGHT, not knowledge. タロ is the smartest-feeling character precisely because it is word-BESOTTED (collects words like treasure: 「ことば、つかまえたっ！」), not because it knows answers. The peer feel comes from shared discovery ("we caught a word"), the pet feel would come from タロ depending on the child for care. Spec: the child and タロ co-capture words; タロ never asks to be fed/petted.

5. SILHOUETTE/READ-TEST ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (honour the gate): at 1-bit 24px, タロ is identifiable by body-blob + tipped-cap notch ALONE (glow is colour, not silhouette — so it must read without it). 6yo+parent read-test pass = (a) 6yo names the current emotion correctly from face alone within 2s using only happy/surprise/"trying" (the legible set), (b) parent describes it as "encouraging," never "anxious/creepy" (the Labubu unease that sells to Gen Z FAILS the 6yo parent-trust test — flag below).
designs.the_one_thing: Make the honey-amber pride-glow the SINGLE signature AND wire it 1:1 to the child's correct answer, so the one viral micro-behavior (Mirumi's lesson) and the one teaching signal (合格率/mastery) are the SAME thing. A 6yo doesn't read text or subtle faces — they read "I made タロ glow." That single causal loop is simultaneously the shareable IP beat, the readable-emotion teaching channel, and the structural lock that keeps タロ from ever grading or defining a word. PEDAGOGY FLAG against virality-at-all-costs: do NOT borrow Labubu's "anxious/ugly-cute unease" — that appeal is Gen-Z-collector psychology and reads as scary/sad to a 6yo and untrustworthy to a parent. タロ's startle must always resolve into bravery+warmth within the same beat; warmth must dominate. Virality for THIS product = warmth + one child-caused glow, not edginess.

critiques.persona: Brutally honest 2026 viral/IP skeptic, scroll-test lens (Mirumi/Twinkle-Twinkle/Crybaby benchmark).
critiques.viral_on_character_alone: CONDITIONAL — strong love-engine (flinch→peek→earned glow) but NOT yet viral on character alone, because the static silhouette is rented from a removable prop, not owned by anatomy. As assembled: a stranger screenshots the GLOW MOMENT (a behavior), not タロ-at-rest (a bean in a hat). Fix the anatomy and it crosses into yes; ship as-is and it's a forgettable healing-plush with a good animation.
critiques.worst_flaw: The signature lives in a HAT, so the silhouette is a costume, not a species. Strip the detective cap in the panel's own 24px 1-bit test and what remains is a generic round bean + two low eyes + two small ears = every Sonny Angel / Jellycat / Momiji / Etsy healing-plush on the 2026 shelf. A cap is the most copied, least ownable signature in IP (a genre prop you can put on any blob), whereas the actual 2026 breakouts the deck cites win on a BODY anomaly you can't file off (Mirumi's eye-on-a-stem peek, Labubu's serrated grin, Pikachu's tail). The panel correctly killed cap+tail+glow+monocle, then crowned the one survivor that is itself a removable cliché — the committee-creature trap re-entering through the back door. "Redrawable in 4 strokes" was mistaken for "ownable"; a child drawing "circle + tilted brim" is drawing a HAT, not タロ. On character alone, at rest, in a thumbnail, on a shelf, タロ is a bean in a hat — and strangers don't screenshot a bean in a hat.
critiques.fix: Move the sole signature OFF the cap and INTO the anatomy: give タロ ONE strange body part that IS the word-besotted-but-startled contradiction — oversized, MISMATCHED listening ears. One ear permanently cocked/perked UP (word-hunting, always straining to catch a word = besotted), the other folded/DOWN (the startle frozen into the body). This is a true SHAPE that survives 1-bit 24px with the cap GONE, so タロ is still タロ bareheaded (species, not costume); it freezes the brave/startled duality into a STATIC thumbnail (no animation needed to read it); it amplifies the existing love-engine (both ears flatten on ビクッ, both prick on つかまえたっ); stays 6yo-redrawable (one tall ear, one folded) and parent-warm. Demote the detective cap to the SECOND thing you notice — flavor on a creature that's already unmistakable. Touches nothing locked: glow stays the #E0A85A earned state, cap stays tipped over one eye, voice/world/pedagogy untouched. Acceptance test: if タロ is still recognizably タロ with the hat removed, you have an IP; if he's a blob without it, you have a Halloween costume — ship only once he passes hatless.

critiques.persona: Dual lens. (1) RINA, age 6 (年長): can read ひらがな slowly, cannot read English, decides "好き/きらい" in under 2 seconds from the FACE, wants a small friend who needs her, screenshots/redraws what she loves, gets bored if nothing reacts to HER. (2) Her MOM, 38: pays the ¥999, scans for "creepy / gacha / 課金ガチャ / is this safe," wants to see her kid actually learning 英検 not just hugging a mascot, trusts warm-but-competent, distrusts anything that smells like a collectible-gambling funnel.
critiques.viral_on_character_alone: CONDITIONAL — strong on paper, NOT yet true, and one design choice is actively capping the ceiling. WHY: The spec is genuinely 2026-correct — the Mirumi/Twinkle-Twinkle/Crybaby evidence all converges on "one warm shape + one child-caused micro-reaction," and タロ's brave-but-startled→glow loop nails that exact mechanism. A 6yo CAN love "I made my tiny brave friend glow" and CAN redraw "two circles, one tilted cap." That part is real. BUT three things gate virality from the dual lens: (a) RIGHT NOW the rendered character is literally a slime (lib/features/quest/quest_screen.dart:330 maps 'タロ'→assets/art/masters/slime.webp; npc_slime_color/grey.webp are the in-scene art) — so the actual shipped タロ is a generic blob that fails its OWN 16px silhouette gate. Virality-on-character-alone is currently FALSE in the build; it only exists in the bible. (b) The "viral beat" (glow) is INVISIBLE in every shareable static surface — silhouette, sticker shape, plush, favicon, screenshot thumbnail — by the spec's own rule ("glow is value not shape"). The thing meant to go viral is the one thing a still frame can't carry; it only lives in a ~0.6s animation a stranger has to play the app to see. Twinkle-Twinkle/Crybaby go viral as a STILL OBJECT on a shelf. タロ's hero moment is invisible at rest. (c) The brave-but-startled flinch is the genuinely ownable, screenshot-able, meme-able beat (Mirumi proved exactly this) — yet the spec demotes it to "an entry emotion, budgeted, only on new words" and elevates the glow (a generic warm-light reward every kids app has) to THE signature. The team optimized the wrong micro-moment as the IP. Verdict: viral-capable archetype, mis-prioritized signature, and not actually rendered yet.
critiques.worst_flaw: THE SIGNATURE IS INVISIBLE AT THE ONLY MOMENT THAT GOES VIRAL — and the build ships a slime in its place. Unpack the dual lens: RINA decides in 2 seconds from a STILL face on a screen (and from the sticker/plush/thumbnail her friend shows her). The spec deliberately makes タロ's identity-and-warmth split across two channels that never coexist in a still image: the CAP carries identity (silhouette) and the GLOW carries warmth+the-payoff (color-state, invisible in silhouette). So every static surface a 6yo or a feed ever sees — favicon, sticker outline, plush on a shelf, a screenshot, a redraw — shows ONLY a small body + tilted cap, which at rest is "a generic chibi in a hat": competent-looking but emotionally COLD and un-screenshot-worthy. The warmth and the love-engine and the entire "this is why you protect it" payload exist solely inside a 0.6s in-app animation nobody outside the app sees. That is the precise inverse of how Twinkle-Twinkle/Mirumi/Crybaby went viral — those are warm as a FROZEN OBJECT. MOM's lens compounds it from the other side: the one thing the spec treats as the collectible flywheel (ことばコレクション earned stickers) is fine, but the static merch she'd actually see on a shelf is the cold capped blob with no visible warmth — it reads as "branded mascot," not "thing my kid will cry to own," so it won't drive the word-of-mouth pull that justifies the IP bet. And underneath both: the SHIPPED build literally renders a slime named タロ (quest_screen.dart:330), which the project's own [[distinct-identity-not-ip-copy]] memo flags as a DQ-monster identity it's trying to escape — so today the answer to "does the child instantly love タロ" is "she sees a slime," full stop. Secondary but real: the DECISION doc's own latest art-gate audits keep smuggling back puppy anatomy (muzzle/floppy ear/Q-tail, lines 102-130) to FIX the failed silhouette — which is the codebase admitting the cap-only silhouette doesn't actually read as a lovable specific creature, only as "a hat." The hat is an accessory, not a soul; a 6yo bonds to a FACE and a BODY, not a prop.
critiques.fix: Make the WARMTH structural (in the body/face), not a transient effect — and promote the FLINCH, not the glow, to the shareable signature. Concretely: (1) Give タロ ONE warm-at-rest structural cue baked into the silhouette+face so a STILL frame is instantly lovable without animation: oversized low-set worried-hopeful eyes + a small matte blush + a soft rounded body that already reads "needs protecting." This survives 24px 1-bit AND carries warmth in a frozen thumbnail/sticker/plush — fixing the "cold capped blob" failure. The cap stays the identity SHAPE; the FACE carries the warmth. Two cues, one silhouette, not a committee. (2) Re-rank the micro-moments: the brave→STARTLE→peek (the Mirumi flinch, the びくっ→…っ) becomes THE ownable, meme-able, sticker-#1 signature beat — it is readable as a still pose (cap jolted over both eyes, body rigid), it's the "ヤダ"-class panic sticker that actually drives shares, and it's the love-engine made visible WITHOUT animation. The honey-glow stays as the earned in-app reward payoff (keep it — it's a great 合格率 signal), but stop pretending a generic warm light is the viral IP; the FLINCH is. (3) Ship the real art before any further wiring: kill the slime placeholder — quest_screen.dart:330 and the npc_slime_*.webp pair must point at actual タロ art that passes the project's own 16px-cap-dome silhouette gate + a real RINA-and-mom read-test (6yo names the emotion from face-alone in 2s using only happy/surprise/「がんばってる」; mom calls it "encouraging," never "creepy" or "gacha"). Until that render exists, "does the child love タロ" is untestable and the honest answer is "she loves/ignores a slime." (4) Parent-trust lock (already mostly right, keep it): hold the line on deterministic earned ことばコレクション (Pokémon-GO model) and zero sealed-random/spend-to-reveal — the June-2026 blind-box backlash makes any gacha smell a hard parent-trust kill; the spec already dodges this, do not let merch reintroduce it.
